target_files_to_update: [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] (verified with openpyxl/Excel and optional CSV export)

planned_changes: Open [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] in a standard environment (Excel or Python with openpyxl) and verify that formula cells exist and compute the values indicated in the memo. Record a brief verification artifact: either a CSV export of the computed ranges or a screenshot of the relevant sheet ranges.

Add a plain-text CSV export of the principal sheet(s) (e.g., finite_capacity_audit.csv) containing inputs and computed outputs (baseline durations, cumulatives, lateness, scenario multipliers) and include it in the attempt deliverables.

Regenerate expert_memo.md without any appended metadata; ensure the produced file matches the runtime trace byte-size or update the trace if regenerated. Re-run artifact preview to confirm the preview contains only the memo text.

Explicitly state and correct the direction/sign of O3 in both the workbook and memo (e.g., 'O3: machine capacity change = -25% => capacity reduced by 25% (multiply capacity by 0.75)'). Recompute scenarios if the sign interpretation changes outcomes and document differences.
Update the memo to include a short 'verification' section listing the exact cell ranges used for key inputs and outputs and referencing the attached CSV/screenshot verification artifact.
Re-run the artifact preview generation and confirm no parsing errors. If environment limitations persist, include both .xlsx and CSV exports for automated preview resilience.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Remove verification uncertainty introduced by preview parsing failure and memo preview corruption; clarify ambiguous option sign and attach verification artifacts so semantic correctness is independently confirmable.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Artifacts and memo are present and the grader/verifier results support a passing artifact-contract check. However automated previewing could not parse the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable) and the memo preview appears concatenated with metadata; the semantic pass therefore relies on trusting the agent trace and memo rather than an independently parsed workbook. Recommend targeted fixes to remove residual verification risk and improve reproducibility.
